full_response:
We work in the rest frame of the Higgs boson.  Let its 4-momentum be  
P_h^μ = (E_h/c, 0, 0, 0) with E_h = m_h c^2 = 125 GeV.  After decay into two photons, label their 4-momenta as  
k_1^μ = (E_1/c, p⃗_1),   k_2^μ = (E_2/c, p⃗_2),  
with E_i = |p⃗_i| c since photons are massless.

By 4-momentum conservation:  
P_h^μ = k_1^μ + k_2^μ.  
Breaking into energy and 3-momentum parts gives  
(1) Energy: E_1 + E_2 = E_h = m_h c^2,  
(2) Momentum: p⃗_1 + p⃗_2 = 0  ⇒  p⃗_2 = −p⃗_1,  so |p⃗_1| = |p⃗_2|.

But for each photon, E_i = |p⃗_i| c, so from (2) we get E_1 = E_2.  Plugging into (1):  
2 E_1 = m_h c^2  ⇒  E_1 = E_2 = (m_h c^2)/2 = (125 GeV)/2 = 62.5 GeV.

Thus in the Higgs rest frame each photon has energy 62.5 GeV.  

Final Answer:  
\boxed{E_{\gamma1} = E_{\gamma2} = 62.5\;\text{GeV}}  

Confidence: 100%
